title: pomaderris canescens
intro: Pomaderris canescens is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae, commonly known as the white pomaderris. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, where its leaves and bark possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves and bark of the plant have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, providing several health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Pomaderris canescens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and is also used in traditional Aboriginal medicine to make poultices and salves for topical application.
